commonIssues: Antirrhinum rust is a significant disease — avoid overhead watering and remove infected foliage
Powdery mildew, downy mildew, fungal leaf spots, wilts, and root rots in poorly drained or humid conditions
Bloom stalls and plants languish in summer heat — performs best in cool seasons (spring/fall)
Tall cut-flower cultivars need staking or netting; dwarf bedding types do not
Deadhead spent spikes to prolong flowering and prevent the plant from setting seed and dying back
A tender short-lived perennial usually grown as a hardy annual; tolerates light frost but is killed by very hard freezes

source.citation: North Carolina State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox — Antirrhinum majus: full sun (6+ hours) to partial shade (2–6 hours); moist, rich, well-drained soil; good container plant; tall plants may need staking; low drought tolerance, languishes in summer heat; zones 7a–10b; significant rust susceptibility.
NC State Plant Toolbox — 'low drought tolerance' and need for consistently moist, well-drained soil informs the short watering interval and low drying tolerance.

Clemson HGIC, 'Snapdragons for Fall Blooms' — plant in full to part sun; water on a regular basis; fertilize with an all-purpose fertilizer after cutting back; taller varieties require support; perennial only in zones 9+; deadhead and cut tall types back to 6 inches after flowering.

University of Illinois Extension, Flowers: Snapdragon — hardy annual, very cold tolerant, performs in cool seasons until a very hard frost kills it; full sun to light shade; moist well-drained soil; blooms May–October; tall cultivars may need staking.
University of Illinois Extension, Container Gardens: Fertilizing — reapplying fertilizer every two weeks provides adequate nutrition for most containers (basis for the 14-day feeding interval).

Mississippi State University Extension, 'Snapdragon (Antirrhinum majus) for the Farmer Florist' — full sun to partial shade; space 4–12 in; ideal night temps above 60F (16C); drip irrigation preferred since overhead watering increases disease; major diseases rust, botrytis, powdery/downy mildew, root rots; pests aphids, whiteflies, leafminers.

RHS, Antirrhinum majus — full sun, well-drained soil; hardiness H2 (1–5C minimum for reliable overwintering of tender stock); flowers late spring to autumn; susceptible to antirrhinum rust, powdery mildews, aphids; deadheading prolongs flowering.

Confidence note: no single extension page gives an exact container-watering interval or minimum pot volume for snapdragons. baseIntervalDays=3 is a conservative reading of 'regular, consistent moisture / ~1 inch per week, more in containers' guidance; recommendedMinLiters=4 corresponds to a standard 8-inch (20 cm) pot commonly used for bedding annuals; coldHardyMinC=-5 is a conservative light-frost tolerance consistent with 'very cold tolerant hardy annual killed by very hard frost' (Illinois) and RHS H2; heatStressC=27 reflects 'languishes in summer heat' (NCSU) without a published exact threshold.
